final_answer:
1. Lock the invariant: one operator address may control exactly one active validator slot at a time (`one operator = one active validator`).
2. Add one keeper-level conflict check helper that returns whether an operator already maps to a different validator ID, with `excludeValID` support for self-updates.
3. Enforce that helper in the proposal-open path of `ApproveValidator` before creating a new nonce/snapshot.
4. Enforce that helper in every state-changing admission path that can introduce or reactivate a validator identity (`validator-join`, relevant stake lifecycle paths, and any operator-change path).
5. Enforce the same check against pending admissions so duplicates cannot be queued in parallel and finalized later.
6. Add a chain-upgrade audit step that scans existing validator state and pending admissions; abort upgrade if any operator maps to multiple validator IDs.
7. Add deterministic, explicit errors for conflicts so operators see exactly which validator IDs caused rejection.
8. Add regression tests: duplicate operator admission rejected, non-duplicate admission still succeeds, and snapshot-vote flow still finalizes under normal validator churn.
9. Add a query/debug surface for `operator -> validatorID` mapping and conflict visibility for operations.
10. Run full `x/stake` tests and targeted admission-flow tests; only close when all pass and upgrade audit reports zero conflicts.
